key_insight: ClinRAG-GRAPH combines multimodal breast cancer data, graph learning, and evidence retrieval to improve pre-treatment prediction of pathologic complete response after neoadjuvant chemotherapy.
clinical_relevance: If validated further, the approach could help stratify patients earlier and support more personalized treatment decisions before therapy starts.
method: Builds an intra-patient clinical-prior graph from DCE-MRI, clinical variables, and biopsy biomarkers
Uses a prior-guided relation-aware graph convolutional network for multimodal representation learning
Adds dual-branch domain-adversarial learning plus LLM-driven subgraph retrieval for robustness and interpretability
evidence_signal: In multicenter validation, the model achieved AUC 0.815 on the internal test set and 0.774/0.712 on two external test sets.
watch_item: External performance is lower than internal performance, so broader prospective validation and careful handling of center-specific MRI heterogeneity are still needed.
paper_title: ClinRAG-GRAPH: Clinical-prior Retrieval-Augmented Graph Model with Domain Adversarial Learning for Breast pCR Prediction
